mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in existing home sales and new housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in residential demand, which reduces construction sector revenues and consumer wealth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in housing indicators fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate output, which translates into higher near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 70% of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.35 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary policy, increasing equity valuation risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.35 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor market improvement signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio for 20-day forward returns across all expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate profit margins, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases over a 10-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in factory output and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The production decline threshold fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a hit rate above 50% in backtesting.
